coreTruthSet.description: Fire practice is framed as maintenance discipline, not fuel-maximizing intensity, with micro-adjustments rather than forceful intervention.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: The fire was not something to feed. It was something to maintain.

coreTruthSet.description: The house is portrayed as a thermal-memory system that holds and redistributes heat, scent, and labor traces across time.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: The house held heat differently ... it settled into the walls, into the ground, into the objects that lived there.

coreTruthSet.description: Domestic order is described as intentional placement shaped by repeated use rather than decorative arrangement.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Nothing was arranged. Everything was placed.

coreTruthSet.description: Training occurs through sensory literacy (texture, heat, smell, rhythm) and embodied repetition rather than abstract explanation.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Ground corn and water ... thick ... beginning to shift ... the clay was warm to the touch.

settingAnchors.name: Hearth-Centered Household Workspace
settingAnchors.description: Curved-pole, cane-clay domestic interior organized around a low central hearth and continuous productive rhythm.

continuityConstraints.title: Maintenance-Not-Force House Law
continuityConstraints.text: Core household systems remain stable through small continuous adjustments rather than episodic force.

continuityConstraints.title: Correction as Placement
continuityConstraints.text: Corrective speech places the learner back into system rhythm; authority is enacted through alignment, not volume.

continuityConstraints.title: No-Wasted-Motion Discipline
continuityConstraints.text: Labor legitimacy is encoded in efficient motion and contained sound; waste marks misalignment with house order.

openResearchGaps.title: What thresholds trigger correction versus silent observation in novice household training?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed to calibrate authority tone and learning progression.

openResearchGaps.title: What labor rotation rules determine who tends fire, grain, sorting, and repair work across age and status?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed for continuity in domestic scenes and role assignment realism.

openResearchGaps.title: How often are cane/clay wall repairs and hearth adjustments made across seasonal cycles?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed to ground environmental wear-and-maintenance detail in recurring practice.
